headline: 19 men charged in mass rapes case among 'libertines' in France
description: A man identified as Christophe B. is accused of having forced six former partners into extremely violent sexual encounters with dozens of men recruited online over a period of more than 20 years, under the guise of 'libertinism.'

headline: Andy Burnham declared leader of Britain's Labour Party, set to be PM on Monday
Germany to join French military nuclear exercise, Merz says
French regulators order block of Polymarket's website
description: The French gambling regulator said the site's continued availability constituted advertising. A ban on financial transactions to the site was already in place.

headline: Trump delivers conspiracy-tinged speech on US elections and accuses China of interference
description: In a televised address on Thursday, the US president contested, once again, the 2020 US presidential election results, referencing a so-called 'deep state' that allegedly hid evidence of Chinese meddling.

headline: Behind the scenes of Macron's last G7, as told by photographer Kamil Zihnioglu
description: There is protocol, and then there is Donald Trump. That, in essence, is what photographer Kamil Zihnioglu took away from his three days covering the G7 summit in Evian-les-Bains for Le Monde.

headline: The troubling Russian connections of a 'European' password manager
description: Although it presents itself as a Spanish company, Passwork was actually developed in Russia and operates with a license from Russia's FSB domestic intelligence agency. The situation raises major security questions for its customers, including several French companies.

'It's Mad Max on the ocean': The cocaine-loaded speedboats racing to Europe's shores
description: In the Atlantic, hundreds of powerful go-fast boats, custom-built to transport tons of cocaine, retrieve shipments from vessels traveling commercial routes between South America, Africa and Europe, according to an investigation by Le Monde in partnership with NDR, WDR, Süddeutsche Zeitung, NRC, IrpiMedia and The Washington Post.
byline: Liselotte Mas,  Elsa Delmas and  Thomas Saintourens

headline: 'We were meant to take as many jewels as we could': The confessions of two Louvre burglars
description: After months of silence, two men suspected of stealing the royal jewels described their involvement in detail during new interrogations in June. According to their statements, they were hired to carry out the heist for '€15,000 to €25,000.'

headline: Visualize the full extent of wildfires across Europe in recent weeks
description: The burned areas across the European continent have already far surpassed levels seen in recent years and are now approaching the maximum recorded in 2025.
